response.justification: A seasoned, versatile engineer (15+ years) with strong distributed-systems, low-latency, and event-driven experience, but the role's core requirement — modern, hands-on C++/STL development — is barely present. The only substantive C++ work (National Instruments, ~6 months) was a decade ago, and there is no evidence of MQTT, message brokers, industrial protocols (Modbus/OPC UA), or IEC 62443/SDL security practice. Adjacent strengths (concurrency, high-throughput services, mentoring) make this borderline rather than a clear pass.
strengths: Proven high-throughput, low-latency, event-driven systems work (millions of req/sec)
Broad senior/staff experience with strong testing and craftsmanship (TDD, CI/CD)
Demonstrated mentoring and teaching ability (workshops, dojos, courses)
concerns: Minimal and dated C++/STL experience; primary stack is JS/TS and DLang
No industrial automation, MQTT, message brokers, or IEC 62443/SDL security background
No Azure or IoT protocol exposure (Modbus, OPC UA, DNP3, etc.)
